tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Nearly all fail-to-pass tests are pure text matching: they read file contents and check for substring presence (string(FIND ...) in CMake scripts, or 'assert X in content.lower()'). No test actually runs a CMake configure, builds anything, or verifies behavioral correctness. The CMake _cmake_check helper just reads files and searches for strings — it doesn't actually configure or build any project.

test_not_tautological.reason: A stub implementation that simply appends the required strings to the files (e.g., adding a comment containing '-DPORT=JSCOnly', 'JSC_CMAKE_ARGS', 'execute_process', 'add_custom_target(jsc', 'option(WEBKIT_BUILD_TYPE', 'TARGET jsc', 'find_package(ICU') would pass all fail-to-pass tests without implementing any actual build logic. The tests only check for string presence, not functional behavior.

behavior_in_tests.reason: All behaviors described in instruction.md are tested: JSC configure integration, WEBKIT_BUILD_TYPE option, jsc custom target, jsc dependency in BuildBun, system ICU usage, documentation updates for simplified workflow, removal of old manual steps, and retention of prebuilt WebKit support.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are well-organized with clear section comments (Gates, Fail-to-pass CMake changes, Fail-to-pass Documentation, Pass-to-pass regression, Pass-to-pass repo tests), descriptive function names, and docstrings explaining each check.

structured_data_schema.reason: This task involves CMake build configuration and documentation changes, not structured data output like JSON/CSV/API/DB.
